title: psorospermum bullatum
intro: Psorospermum bullatum, also known as the stinking chamomile, is a plant species in the family Melastomataceae. It is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments. Herbal preparations of Psorospermum bullatum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
